pattern: Precompute a prefix array of the first 'violation' position for each index, then answer each range query with a single comparison.
hints: A subarray is not special when any two adjacent elements share the same parity. Precompute where violations occur.
Build a prefix-min (or bad-pair flag) array: for each index i mark whether nums[i] and nums[i-1] share the same parity, then use a prefix sum to query ranges in O(1).
Compute bad[i] = 1 if nums[i] and nums[i-1] have the same parity, else 0. Build prefix sums; subarray [l, r] is special iff the sum of bad[l+1..r] equals zero.

edge_inputs.description: whole array has a parity violation
multiple queries mixing true and false
query spanning exactly the violation boundary
same query repeated expecting same answer

stress.description: large random array with many random queries
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(2, size)
    nums = [rng.randint(1, 100000) for _ in range(n)]
    q = max(1, size // 10)
    queries = []
    for _ in range(q):
        l = rng.randint(0, n - 1)
        r = rng.randint(l, n - 1)
        queries.append([l, r])
    return [nums, queries]
